dimensions.reason: Each retained record states why its assignment was selected relative to dependencies and blockers.
Every record retains an immutable starting commit in the versioned workflow contract.
Every record retains an immutable ending commit in the versioned workflow contract.
Commit-scoped CI states and run identifiers are retained in the three workflow records.
The repair and blocked-reassignment records retain failure categories, summaries and resolutions.
Each workflow record retains pending human decisions and claim boundaries.
The scheduled-run unit is represented consistently as one retained agent turn per record.
The historical run logs do not provide a complete, independently auditable tool-call transcript, so recorded counts cannot yet support comparative claims.
No contemporaneous human time log exists for the first batch; zero-valued record fields must not be interpreted as measured absence of human labor.
The retained records declare zero paid generation and zero external API cost for these deterministic increments.
Accepted and revised output decisions are retained with reasons where the contract requires them.

claim_boundary: This declaration exposes which first-batch fields are evidenced and which cannot be reconstructed. It does not validate the accuracy of every historical value, estimate total human labor, establish longitudinal coverage, or support workflow-superiority claims.
